input.title: Why I moved from Pub/Sub to Redis Streams
input.content:
We started with Redis Pub/Sub for async enrichment. Worked fine — until our AI worker crashed and we lost 3 hours of events. Pub/Sub has no persistence; offline subscribers miss messages forever.

Redis Streams fixes this:
- XADD appends to a log; data lives until you XTRIM it
- Consumer groups track per-consumer offsets (XREADGROUP)
- XACK explicitly commits; unacked messages come back on reconnect

Migration was small: swap Publish/Subscribe bodies, keep our interface unchanged. Worth it.
